# Peter Parts Visual Manual

This manual defines the visual elements that represent Peter Parts.

Its purpose is to ensure the brand identity is applied consistently across all formats and contexts.

It serves as a reference document, not a list of restrictions.

The more these guidelines are respected, the stronger and more recognizable the brand will become over time.

## 1. Brand Overview

- Name: Peter Parts
- Service: Specialized spare parts and maintenance for KitchenAid

## 2. Logo Guidelines

### Core Concept

- The Peter Parts logo combines two elements: the letter "P" and the KitchenAid balloon whisk integrated within the symbol.
- The silhouette of the mixer is implied in the shape of the "P," linking the brand directly to the product it represents.
- There is a main version and several variants for different applications.

### Clear Space (Safe Area)

- The minimum space around the logo equals the height of the letter "S" from the word "PARTS."
- This space must not be invaded by any other graphic or textual element.

### Incorrect Uses

To maintain brand integrity, always avoid the following:

- Do not deform: Never stretch or compress the logo.
- Do not rotate: The logo must always remain in a horizontal position.
- Do not change colors: Use only the official palette.
- Do not add effects: Avoid shadows, gradients, or additional borders.
- Do not place on complex backgrounds: The logo requires sufficient contrast.
- Do not reduce below minimum size: Minimum width for printing is 2 cm.

## 3. Color Palette

| Color Name | Hex Code | Usage | RGB | CMYK |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Bikini Red | #D91E36 | Primary color | 217, 30, 54 | 0/86/75/15 |
| Pungent Red | #630E19 | Secondary color | 99, 14, 25 | 0/86/75/61 |
| Black | #1A1714 | Texts / dark backgrounds | 26, 23, 20 | 0/11/23/90 |
| White | #FFFFFF | Background / contrast | 255, 255, 255 | 0/0/0/0 |

## 4. Typography

- Logo Font (Schrifted Round): Used exclusively in the logo and not for communication text.
- Primary Typography (Open Sans): Used for primary texts, titles, and hero sections where attention and visual weight are needed.
- Secondary Typography (Lato Font Family): Used for secondary texts, descriptions, captions, and digital communication requiring visual lightness.

## 5. Communication Tone

Peter Parts speaks as a trusted specialist, not a generic store.

The tone is direct and technical when necessary, but always accessible, conveying expertise without being arrogant.

- Reliable: Speaks with certainty and demonstrates expertise.
- Specialized: Knows KitchenAid deeply and does not sell random items.
- Close: Avoids unnecessary technicalities and remains clear and direct.
- Careful: Understands that the customer values their machine.
